description_long: This object is a large, whole cured ham with a natural, irregular shape and a tapered end resembling a pig's leg. The exterior is a mottled tan and brown color, with visible stamps and markings typical of traditional cured meats. The surface texture appears slightly rough and dry, with some areas showing a waxy sheen and others a powdery finish. The cut face of the ham reveals a pinkish-red interior with white fat marbling, indicating a well-cured product. There are no visible grill marks, garnishes, or plating elements present. The object is solid and dense, with a clear distinction between the cured meat and the fat layers.
description: A large, whole cured ham with a tan-brown exterior, visible stamps, and a pink, marbled cut face. The surface is rough and dry, with no grill marks or garnishes.
description_view_0: Side view showing the broad, rounded body of the ham with tan and brown mottling and visible stamps.
description_view_1: Tapered end view highlighting the pointed, narrow shank and the natural skin texture.
description_view_2: Opposite side view similar to view 0, with more visible fat and a slightly different angle on the stamps.
description_view_3: Cut face view displaying the pinkish-red meat and white fat marbling, with a smooth, slightly glossy surface.
attribute_grill_marks: No grill marks are visible on the surface of the steak.
attribute_visible_fat_marbling: Moderate fat marbling is visible, mostly concentrated near the cut face and edges.
